resources.string: Shows Auto Brightness debug flash messages system-wide (over other apps). This optional service only draws the message overlays — it never reads screen content. Enable it only if you want the diagnostic flashes visible while using other apps.
Grant \'Modify system settings\' to control brightness
Manual override active — tap Resume to continue
Auto Brightness paused — tap Resume to continue
Live data may be stale — the monitor may have stopped responding.
Circadian using a sun position cached %1$d day(s) ago — turn on Location briefly to refresh.
Circadian using default sun times (no location) — turn on Location briefly for accurate sunrise/sunset.
Showing cached sun events from %1$d day(s) ago. Turn on Location briefly — or enable the IP fallback below — to refresh sunrise/sunset.
No location yet, so circadian is using default sun times. Turn on Location briefly — or enable the IP fallback below — for accurate sunrise/sunset.
No write access — tap to set up
You changed the brightness yourself, so auto-control is paused. Resume to hand control back.
Snap the screen back to the automatic brightness and clear a manual change
Couldn\'t open the tile picker on this device
Live brightness, lux, profile and context with a quick service toggle and reset.
This project is a complete, native replacement for Android\'s stock auto-brightness system — a more intelligent, responsive, and customizable brightness experience.
Created by /u/v_uurtjevragen, who built the original in Tasker and authored this native Android rebuild.
Thanks to João Dias, creator of Tasker — the automation app the original Advanced Auto Brightness was built on.

Copyright (c) 2026 /u/v_uurtjevragen

Permission is hereby granted, free of charge, to any person obtaining a copy of this software and associated documentation files (the "Software"), to deal in the Software without restriction, including without limitation the rights to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute, sublicense, and/or sell copies of the Software, and to permit persons to whom the Software is furnished to do so, subject to the following conditions:

The above copyright notice and this permission notice shall be included in all copies or substantial portions of the Software.

THE SOFTWARE IS PROVIDED "AS IS", WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO THE WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE AND NONINFRINGEMENT. IN NO EVENT SHALL THE AUTHORS OR COPYRIGHT HOLDERS BE LIABLE FOR ANY CLAIM, DAMAGES OR OTHER LIABILITY, WHETHER IN AN ACTION OF CONTRACT, TORT OR OTHERWISE, ARISING FROM, OUT OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH THE SOFTWARE OR THE USE OR OTHER DEALINGS IN THE SOFTWARE.

This project is a complete replacement for your phone\'s built-in auto brightness. Its goal is a smoother, more intelligent, and completely customizable experience that adapts to light the way you want it to.

• **Grant Permissions:** on first launch, Tideo guides you through granting notifications and write-settings permissions (essential for the app to function).
• **Activate the Service:** flip the master switch on the Dashboard. A persistent notification confirms Tideo is active and disables Android's native auto-brightness.
• **Set Your Boundaries:** Menu → Misc. Adjust Min and Max Brightness — the absolute darkest/brightest the screen will ever get.
[TIP] For OLED screens, set Min Brightness to a small value (5 or 10) instead of 0 to prevent "black crush".

All settings are reached from the Menu. **Curve & Brightness** shapes the core curve; **Reactivity** tunes sensor behaviour; **Misc** holds master limits and animations; **Circadian** scales brightness by time of day; **Super Dimming** handles ultra-low brightness and PWM flicker; **Profiles & Contexts** manage configurations and automatic switching.\n[TIP] Your secret weapon: tap the ⓘ icon next to any setting\'s label for a detailed explanation.

A three-part formula:
• **Zone 1 (darkness to dim):** Zone 1 scaling is the main low-light control; Zone 1 End is where "dark" becomes "dim".
• **Zone 2 (dim to medium):** Zone 2 scaling and offset shape the curve for common indoor light — use the graph to see the transition.
• **Zone 3 (bright):** handled automatically, smoothly approaching maximum brightness.
• **Automatic Curve Fitting:** with override detection on, Tideo saves your manual points. Gather data in ≥9 lighting conditions, then run the curve wizard and review on the graph before applying.

A smart dead zone removes fluctuations from sensor noise so the screen reacts only on significant light changes.\n• **Thresholds:** Dark, Dim and Bright control the dead-zone size (higher = more stable).\n• **Curve:** mid and slope control the smooth sensitivity transition between dim and bright.

Save the current config as a named profile and swap between strategies.\n• **Dashboard tracking:** active settings are compared to factory defaults; tuned values appear in gold.\n• **System presets:** Battery Saver and Outdoors are modifiable; the default profile is modifiable but not deletable.

Rules pair triggers with a profile; a matching trigger auto-loads it.
• **Triggers:** App, Time & Day (supports SUNRISE/SUNSET), Battery, Location (geofence), and Wi-Fi.
• **Conflicts:** higher Priority (1–100) wins; ties broken by more triggers (more specific).
• **Pausing:** manually loading a profile pauses the context engine; resume from the Profiles screen or by cycling the screen off/on.
[TIP] Location rules cost battery — prefer Wi-Fi rules over geofences where you can.

• **Dynamic Scaling (Circadian):** adjusts the whole curve by time of day relative to sunrise/sunset.
• **Super Dimming & PWM-sensitive mode:** go darker than Android's minimum via elevated access or a screen-filter overlay; PWM-sensitive mode fixes hardware brightness at a flicker-free floor and dims further with a software overlay.
[WARN] Set the PWM threshold to the **lowest** value that eliminates flicker. Too high keeps pixels at high power while dimmed by overlay, accelerating screen aging / burn-in.
• **Manual Override:** adjusting the slider pauses Tideo and posts a notification; tap Resume.
• **Panic Button:** shake the device vertically while upside down to reset, stop automation and force max brightness (S.O.S. vibration confirms).
• **Quick Settings tile:** pause/resume or toggle the service.

• **Brightness graph:** teal line = your curve, blue dots = manual override points, gold line = reference.
• **Reactivity graph:** the stability curve and the light change required to trigger an update.
• **Other graphs:** Circadian and Super Dimming visualize behaviour over time and brightness.
• **Power Draw calibration (Tools):** an experimental tool that steps through brightness levels and measures current to build a power curve.

• **Apply:** makes changes active immediately, but not written to disk — lost on a profile load or app restart.
• **Save (Profiles screen):** writes current settings to the selected profile — the only way to make changes permanent.
• **Reset:** reverts the current tab to defaults.
[TIP] After editing multiple tabs, save to your active profile; if using the context engine, overwrite the relevant profile or your changes are replaced when a rule fires.
Enjoy your new intelligent auto brightness!

Measures how much power your screen draws at each brightness level: the display fills with white and steps through brightness while Tideo samples battery current. Best with Airplane Mode on and the charger unplugged.

For accurate results:

1. Enable Airplane Mode (important)
2. Close background apps
3. Do not touch the screen during the test
4. Unplug the charger

The screen will turn white and sweep through brightness levels for ~1–2 minutes.
Battery current sensor not available on this device
Please unplug the charger and try again
